meaning: n.投掷,疾行,毛皮vt.剥...的皮,投掷,投击,(雨、雪等)急降,攻击vi.投击,猛落
examples: Some of the younger men began to pelt one another with snowballs.
Look at the sunlight a glowing ribbon draped along the dark pelt of hemlock and pine.
Look at the sunlight, a glowing ribbon draped along the dark pelt of hemlock and pine.
Glasper has gone on to work with Nicholas Payton, Roy Hargrove, Terence Blanchard and Jeremy Pelt.
He had a thick graying beard that looked alarmingly similar to the rabbit pelt.
